lexicon: abg_2012 (Apel & Blix Grimaldi 2012, verbatim - see pipeline/score/lexicon/abg_2012.json and DECISIONS.md). starter_v0 remains in the repo as plumbing only and is no longer used in any output.

documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 5 August 2015, the MPC voted by a majority of 8-1 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.
At its meeting ending on 9 September 2015, the MPC voted by a majority of 8-1 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.
At its meeting ending on 6 October, the MPC voted by a majority of 8-1 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.

At its meeting ending on 3 February 2016, the MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.
At its meeting ending on 16 March 2016, the MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.

At its meeting ending on 15 June 2016 the MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.

documents.decision: maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%, with one member voting for a cut in Bank Rate to 0.25%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 13 July 2016, the MPC voted by a majority of 8-1 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%, with one member voting for a cut in Bank Rate to 0.25%.
Bank of England Minutes of the Monetary Policy Committee Meeting ending on 3 August 2016 9 The Committee voted unanimously in favour of the propositions on Bank Rate and the Term Funding Scheme.
At its meeting ending on 14 September 2016, the MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.25%.
At its meeting ending on 2 November 2016 the Committee voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.25%.
At its meeting ending on 14 December 2016 the Committee voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.25%.
At its meeting ending on 1 February 2017, the Committee voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.25%.

At its meeting ending on 13 September 2017, the MPC voted by a majority of 7-2 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.25%.

documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 1 November 2017, the MPC voted by a majority of 7-2 to increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 0.5%.
At its meeting ending on 13 December 2017, the MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.

At its meeting ending on 20 June 2018, the MPC voted by a majority of 6-3 to maintain Bank Rate at 0.5%.

documents.decision: increase the Bank of England’s holdings of UK government bonds and sterling non-financial investment-grade corporate bonds by £200 billion to a total of £645 billion, financed by the issuance of central bank reserves, and to reduce Bank Rate by 15 basis points to 0.1%
documents.raw_vote_text: It therefore voted unanimously to increase the Bank of England’s holdings of UK government bonds and sterling non-financial investment-grade corporate bonds by £200 billion to a total of £645 billion, financed by the issuance of central bank reserves, and to reduce Bank Rate by 15 basis points to 0.1%.

documents.decision: increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 5.25%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 2 August 2023, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 20 September 2023, the MPC voted by a majority of 5–4 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 1 November 2023, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 13 December 2023, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 31 January 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 20 March 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 8–1 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 8 May 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 7–2 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.
At its meeting ending on 19 June 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 7–2 to maintain Bank Rate at 5.25%.

documents.decision: reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 5%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 31 July 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 5–4 to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 5%.
At its meeting ending on 18 September 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 8–1 to maintain Bank Rate at 5%.

documents.decision: reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.75%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 6 November 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 8–1 to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.75%.
At its meeting ending on 18 December 2024, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to maintain Bank Rate at 4.75%.

documents.decision: reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.5%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 5 February 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 7–2 to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.5%.
At its meeting ending on 19 March 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 8–1 to maintain Bank Rate at 4.5%.

documents.decision: reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.25%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 7 May 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 5–4 to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4.25%.
At its meeting ending on 18 June 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 6–3 to maintain Bank Rate at 4.25%.

documents.decision: reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4%, rather than maintaining it at 4.25%
documents.raw_vote_text: At its meeting ending on 6 August 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 5–4 to reduce Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points, to 4%, rather than maintaining it at 4.25%.
At its meeting ending on 17 September 2025, the MPC voted by a majority of 7–2 to maintain Bank Rate at 4%.
